reasoning: The document has three major defects related to reading order and page break artifacts. Both the LLM fidelity evaluation and programmatic signals independently identified that a sentence spanning pages 1 and 2 is interrupted by retained header/footer text and page break artifacts. With 3 major defects, the document requires human review to correct the reading order and merge the split paragraph.

concerns.analysis: The document contains a repeated running header block at the top of page 2 ('FACT SHEET', 'DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL', and the facility address) that is extracted as normal reading content. This text block interrupts a sentence spanning from the bottom of page 1 to the body content of page 2, placing the title and address between 'Persons wishing to' and 'comment on the proposed permit...'. This breaks the logical reading flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Sentence crossing page 1 to page 2: 'Persons wishing to' connects directly to 'comment on the proposed permit and final remedy...'. Top of page 2 contains the running header 'FACT SHEET...'.
concerns.output_quote: The output places the page 2 header elements directly between the sentence fragments: 'Persons wishing to' is followed by '{"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "FACT SHEET"}' and the rest of the address block, before resuming with '{"type": "paragraph", "text": "comment on the proposed permit..."}'.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 1 of 2
Text split by page break (1 instance(s)): p1→p2: “…y, September 7, 2023. Persons wishing to” → “comment on the proposed permit and final…”
concerns.analysis: split_paragraph_count=1. Paragraphs that span page breaks should be merged into a single continuous element. Split sentences disrupt reading order for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): Page 1 of 2
7/7/2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Merge the sentence spanning from the bottom of page 1 to page 2
Remove retained header, footer, and page number artifacts disrupting the text flow
